item.title: Electronic band structure and anisotropic dielectric response of ${\mathrm{PtS}}_{2}$ from angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, spectroscopic ellipsometry, and density functional theory: An indirect-gap hyperbolic van der Waals semiconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Debasis Dutta, Marcin Rosmus, Grazia Giuseppina Politano, Chia-Nung Kuo, Carlo Rizza, Gianluca D'Olimpio, Chin Shan Lue, Amit Agarwal, and Antonio Politano

By combining angle-resolved photoemission, density functional theory, and variable-angle spectroscopic ellipsometry, we determine the bulk electronic structure and anisotropic optical response of platinum disulfide ($\mathrm{Pt}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$). Momentum-resolved maps collected at 60 and 85 eV rev…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085407] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Debasis Dutta, Marcin Rosmus, Grazia Giuseppina Politano, Chia-Nung Kuo, Carlo Rizza, Gianluca D'Olimpio, Chin Shan Lue, Amit Agarwal, and Antonio Politano

By combining angle-resolved photoemission, density functional theory, and variable-angle spectroscopic ellipsometry, we determine the bulk electronic structure and anisotropic optical response of platinum disulfide (PtS2). Momentum-resolved maps collected at 60 and 85 eV reveal a single holelike bra…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085407] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic band structure and anisotropic dielectric response of ${\mathrm{PtS}}_{2}$ from angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, spectroscopic ellipsometry, and density functional theory: An indirect-gap hyperbolic van der Waals semiconductor
item.dc:creator: Debasis Dutta, Marcin Rosmus, Grazia Giuseppina Politano, Chia-Nung Kuo, Carlo Rizza, Gianluca D'Olimpio, Chin Shan Lue, Amit Agarwal, and Antonio Politano

item.title: Origin of moiré potentials in ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ heterobilayers: Contributions from lattice reconstruction and interlayer charge transfer
item.description:
Author(s): Youwen Wang, Nanya Gao, and Qingjun Tong

Moiré superlattices formed in ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ heterobilayers have emerged as an exciting platform to explore the quantum many-body physics. The key mechanism is the introduction of moiré potentials for the band-edge carriers induced by the lateral modulation of interlayer inte…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085408] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Youwen Wang, Nanya Gao, and Qingjun Tong

Moiré superlattices formed in WS2/WSe2 heterobilayers have emerged as an exciting platform to explore the quantum many-body physics. The key mechanism is the introduction of moiré potentials for the band-edge carriers induced by the lateral modulation of interlayer interactions. This trapping potent…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085408] Published Wed Feb 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Origin of moiré potentials in ${\mathrm{WS}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ heterobilayers: Contributions from lattice reconstruction and interlayer charge transfer
item.dc:creator: Youwen Wang, Nanya Gao, and Qingjun Tong
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Relativistic Mott transition and high-order van Hove singularity in twisted double bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$: Mean-field and functional renormalization group study
item.description:
Author(s): Bilal Hawashin, Julian Kleeschulte, David Kurz, Aiman Al-Eryani, and Michael M. Scherer

Experiments on twisted double bilayer tungsten diselenide have demonstrated that moiré semiconductors can realize a relativistic Mott transition, i.e., a quantum phase transition from a Dirac semimetal to a correlated insulating state, by twist-angle tuning. In addition, signatures of van Hove singu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075107] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bilal Hawashin, Julian Kleeschulte, David Kurz, Aiman Al-Eryani, and Michael M. Scherer

Experiments on twisted double bilayer tungsten diselenide have demonstrated that moiré semiconductors can realize a relativistic Mott transition, i.e., a quantum phase transition from a Dirac semimetal to a correlated insulating state, by twist-angle tuning. In addition, signatures of van Hove singu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075107] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Relativistic Mott transition and high-order van Hove singularity in twisted double bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$: Mean-field and functional renormalization group study
item.dc:creator: Bilal Hawashin, Julian Kleeschulte, David Kurz, Aiman Al-Eryani, and Michael M. Scherer

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Emergence of a Fermi surface in the current-driven hidden state of $1T\text{−}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yuval Nitzav, Roni Anna Gofman Kiassi, Ilay Mangel, Abigail Dishi, Nitzan Ragoler, Sajilesh K. P., Yaron Jarach, Alex Louat, Matthew D. Watson, Cephise Cacho, Irena Feldman, and Amit Kanigel

The origin of the insulating state in $1T−{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$ has long been a subject of debate. A short current pulse transforms this insulating state into a metastable metallic phase. Using micro–angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, we investigate the electronic structure of this phase and u…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085104] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuval Nitzav, Roni Anna Gofman Kiassi, Ilay Mangel, Abigail Dishi, Nitzan Ragoler, Sajilesh K. P., Yaron Jarach, Alex Louat, Matthew D. Watson, Cephise Cacho, Irena Feldman, and Amit Kanigel

The origin of the insulating state in 1T−TaS2 has long been a subject of debate. A short current pulse transforms this insulating state into a metastable metallic phase. Using micro–angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, we investigate the electronic structure of this phase and uncover spatially…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085104] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergence of a Fermi surface in the current-driven hidden state of $1T\text{−}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$

item.title: Spin-polarized scanning tunneling microscopy measurement scheme for determining the quantum geometric tensor
item.description:
Author(s): Shu-Hui Zhang, Jin Yang, Ding-Fu Shao, Jia-Ji Zhu, Wen-Long You, Wen Yang, and Kai Chang

The quantum geometric tensor (QGT) embodies the geometry of the eigenstates of a system's Hamiltonian, and its full characterization across diverse quantum systems is essential. However, it is challenging to characterize the QGT of solid-state systems. Here we present an electric scheme to measure t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085403] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shu-Hui Zhang, Jin Yang, Ding-Fu Shao, Jia-Ji Zhu, Wen-Long You, Wen Yang, and Kai Chang

The quantum geometric tensor (QGT) embodies the geometry of the eigenstates of a system's Hamiltonian, and its full characterization across diverse quantum systems is essential. However, it is challenging to characterize the QGT of solid-state systems. Here we present an electric scheme to measure t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085403] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-polarized scanning tunneling microscopy measurement scheme for determining the quantum geometric tensor

item.title: Ab initio spin Hamiltonians and magnetism of Ce and Yb triangular-lattice compounds
item.description:
Author(s): Leonid V. Pourovskii, Rafael D. Soares, and Alexander Wietek

We calculate the crystal-field splitting, ground-state Kramers doublet, and intersite exchange interactions within the ground-state doublet manifold using an ab initio Hubbard-I based approach for a representative set of Ce and Yb triangular-lattice compounds. These include the putative quantum spin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060401] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Leonid V. Pourovskii, Rafael D. Soares, and Alexander Wietek

We calculate the crystal-field splitting, ground-state Kramers doublet, and intersite exchange interactions within the ground-state doublet manifold using an ab initio Hubbard-I based approach for a representative set of Ce and Yb triangular-lattice compounds. These include the putative quantum spin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060401] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Ab initio spin Hamiltonians and magnetism of Ce and Yb triangular-lattice compounds
item.dc:creator: Leonid V. Pourovskii, Rafael D. Soares, and Alexander Wietek
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Anisotropic anomalous Hall effect in distorted kagome ${\mathrm{GdTi}}_{3}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Avdhesh K. Sharma, Bo Tai, Subhajit Roychowdhury, Premakumar Yanda, Ulrich Burkhardt, Xiaolong Feng, Claudia Felser, and Chandra Shekhar

Here, the authors experimentally observe the anisotropic anomalous Hall effect (AHE) in GdTi3Bi4, despite the isotropic magnetization along respective crystallographic directions. This observation contradicts the conventional scaling of AHE with magnetization. In the presence of spin-orbit coupling and broken time-reversal symmetry within the Gd 4f sublattice, the magnetization direction controls the orbital mixing in the Ti t2g bands. This relocates the Berry curvature hot spots and produces orientation-selective AHE. These findings establish GdTi3Bi4 as a platform for novel quantum phenomena.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060402] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Avdhesh K. Sharma, Bo Tai, Subhajit Roychowdhury, Premakumar Yanda, Ulrich Burkhardt, Xiaolong Feng, Claudia Felser, and Chandra Shekhar

Here, the authors experimentally observe the anisotropic anomalous Hall effect (AHE) in GdTi3Bi4, despite the isotropic magnetization along respective crystallographic directions. This observation contradicts the conventional scaling of AHE with magnetization. In the presence of spin-orbit coupling and broken time-reversal symmetry within the Gd 4f sublattice, the magnetization direction controls the orbital mixing in the Ti t2g bands. This relocates the Berry curvature hot spots and produces orientation-selective AHE. These findings establish GdTi3Bi4 as a platform for novel quantum phenomena.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060402] Published Tue Feb 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Anisotropic anomalous Hall effect in distorted kagome ${\mathrm{GdTi}}_{3}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}$
item.dc:creator: Avdhesh K. Sharma, Bo Tai, Subhajit Roychowdhury, Premakumar Yanda, Ulrich Burkhardt, Xiaolong Feng, Claudia Felser, and Chandra Shekhar

item.title: Inferring the pairing symmetry of the superconducting Sn/Si(111) via angle-resolved terahertz pump spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): Mattia Iannetti, Cesare Tresca, Gianni Profeta, Tommaso Cea, and Lara Benfatto

Doping tin surface epitaxially grown on silicon, Sn/Si(111), with boron atoms yields the appearance of a superconducting (SC) phase below ${T}_{c}∼4−9\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$. Even though the pairing mechanism remains unknown, experimental evidence of chiral $d−\mathrm{wave}$ supercon…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054502] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mattia Iannetti, Cesare Tresca, Gianni Profeta, Tommaso Cea, and Lara Benfatto

Doping tin surface epitaxially grown on silicon, Sn/Si(111), with boron atoms yields the appearance of a superconducting (SC) phase below Tc∼4−9K. Even though the pairing mechanism remains unknown, experimental evidence of chiral d−wave superconductivity has been recently reported, thus ruling out a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054502] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Inferring the pairing symmetry of the superconducting Sn/Si(111) via angle-resolved terahertz pump spectroscopy
item.dc:creator: Mattia Iannetti, Cesare Tresca, Gianni Profeta, Tommaso Cea, and Lara Benfatto
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Interplay of magnetism and correlated electron behavior in the kagome metal $\mathrm{TbN}{\mathrm{b}}_{6}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{n}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Rukshana Pervin, Deng Hu, Mir Basit Hussain, Chao Xiong, Liu Yang, Peng Zhu, Caizhen Li, and Zhiwei Wang

Kagome-based magnetic metals are recognized as exemplary systems for the study of quantum materials, offering a distinctive framework to investigate the interrelation of topologically nontrivial electronic structures, geometrically frustrated magnetic moments ($f≈7$), and strongly correlated electro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075101] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026

Kagome-based magnetic metals are recognized as exemplary systems for the study of quantum materials, offering a distinctive framework to investigate the interrelation of topologically nontrivial electronic structures, geometrically frustrated magnetic moments (f≈7), and strongly correlated electron …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075101] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026

item.title: Symmetry-resolved electronic band structure of quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{HfSe}}_{3}(001)$
item.description:
Author(s): Gauthami Viswan, Bushra Ashraf, Jose Avila, Duy Le, Alexey Lipatov, Takashi Komesu, Alexander Sinitskii, Talat S. Rahman, Maria C. Asensio, and Peter A. Dowben

In this work, the band structure of quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{HfSe}}_{3}$ was investigated with nanospot angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (nano-ARPES) in both the $p$- and $s$-polarization geometries and with density functional theory calculations. ${\mathrm{HfSe}}_{3}$ has a rectangu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075401] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gauthami Viswan, Bushra Ashraf, Jose Avila, Duy Le, Alexey Lipatov, Takashi Komesu, Alexander Sinitskii, Talat S. Rahman, Maria C. Asensio, and Peter A. Dowben

In this work, the band structure of quasi-one-dimensional HfSe3 was investigated with nanospot angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (nano-ARPES) in both the p- and s-polarization geometries and with density functional theory calculations. HfSe3 has a rectangular surface Brillouin zone where the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075401] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-resolved electronic band structure of quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{HfSe}}_{3}(001)$
item.dc:creator: Gauthami Viswan, Bushra Ashraf, Jose Avila, Duy Le, Alexey Lipatov, Takashi Komesu, Alexander Sinitskii, Talat S. Rahman, Maria C. Asensio, and Peter A. Dowben

item.title: Near-field radiative heat transfer mediated by monolayer lateral heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Cheng-Long Zhou, Dong-Xu Li, Gui-Cheng Cui, Yong Zhang, and Hong-Liang Yi

Monolayer lateral heterostructures, an interesting class of nanostructures, have garnered significant attention in optoelectronics and nanoelectronics due to their unique physical properties, yet their radiative heat transfer characteristics remain poorly understood. Here, we take $\mathrm{graphene}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085401] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cheng-Long Zhou, Dong-Xu Li, Gui-Cheng Cui, Yong Zhang, and Hong-Liang Yi

Monolayer lateral heterostructures, an interesting class of nanostructures, have garnered significant attention in optoelectronics and nanoelectronics due to their unique physical properties, yet their radiative heat transfer characteristics remain poorly understood. Here, we take graphene−hBN later…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085401] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026

item.title: Parametric instability of plasma waves in graphene excited by a high-frequency electric field
item.description:
Author(s): V. L. Malevich

The system of Vlasov equations for electrons with a linear Dirac spectrum is solved, and a dispersion equation is derived that describes the influence of a uniform high-frequency electric field on the spectrum and damping rate of two-dimensional terahertz (THz) plasmons in single-layer doped graphen…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085402] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. L. Malevich

The system of Vlasov equations for electrons with a linear Dirac spectrum is solved, and a dispersion equation is derived that describes the influence of a uniform high-frequency electric field on the spectrum and damping rate of two-dimensional terahertz (THz) plasmons in single-layer doped graphen…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085402] Published Mon Feb 02, 2026

item.title: Control of spin scattering via spacer layers in NiFe/nonmagnetic heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): M. V. Bakhmetiev, A. S. Pakhomov, A. D. Morozov, A. I. Chernov, and R. B. Morgunov

We investigate spin-charge conversion in exchange-biased NiFe/IrMn heterostructures, where spin-polarized electrons are generated in the NiFe layer by ferromagnetic resonance and transferred by diffusion into adjacent layers. The conversion of spin current into charge current via the inverse spin Ha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014442] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. V. Bakhmetiev, A. S. Pakhomov, A. D. Morozov, A. I. Chernov, and R. B. Morgunov

We investigate spin-charge conversion in exchange-biased NiFe/IrMn heterostructures, where spin-polarized electrons are generated in the NiFe layer by ferromagnetic resonance and transferred by diffusion into adjacent layers. The conversion of spin current into charge current via the inverse spin Ha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014442] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Control of spin scattering via spacer layers in NiFe/nonmagnetic heterostructures
item.dc:creator: M. V. Bakhmetiev, A. S. Pakhomov, A. D. Morozov, A. I. Chernov, and R. B. Morgunov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Microscopic theory for electron-phonon coupling in twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Ziyan Zhu and Thomas P. Devereaux

A quantitative microscopic description of electron-phonon coupling is a prerequisite for resolving the origin of superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene. Here, the authors provide this fundamental component by developing a first-principles-based continuum theory for arbitrary twist angles. They report that coupling is strongly enhanced near the magic angle and persists up to 1.4° despite the loss of electronic flat bands. The study pinpoints specific phonon branches responsible and identifies a resonance between electronic bandwidth and phonon frequencies as the key condition for strong coupling.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035446] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ziyan Zhu and Thomas P. Devereaux

A quantitative microscopic description of electron-phonon coupling is a prerequisite for resolving the origin of superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene. Here, the authors provide this fundamental component by developing a first-principles-based continuum theory for arbitrary twist angles. They report that coupling is strongly enhanced near the magic angle and persists up to 1.4° despite the loss of electronic flat bands. The study pinpoints specific phonon branches responsible and identifies a resonance between electronic bandwidth and phonon frequencies as the key condition for strong coupling.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035446] Published Fri Jan 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic theory for electron-phonon coupling in twisted bilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Ziyan Zhu and Thomas P. Devereaux
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless phase transitions of the antiferromagnetic Ising model with ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interactions on the kagome lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Yutaka Okabe and Hiromi Otsuka

We investigate the six-state clock universality of the Ising model on the kagome lattice, considering antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor (NN) and ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) interactions. Our comprehensive study employs three approaches: the level-spectroscopy method, Monte Carlo simul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014441] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:

We investigate the six-state clock universality of the Ising model on the kagome lattice, considering antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor (NN) and ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) interactions. Our comprehensive study employs three approaches: the level-spectroscopy method, Monte Carlo simul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014441] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless phase transitions of the antiferromagnetic Ising model with ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interactions on the kagome lattice

item.title: Benchmarking the effective many-body flat-band projected model of magic-angle bilayer graphene against the full tight-binding description
item.description:
Author(s): Miguel Sánchez Sánchez and Tobias Stauber

We benchmark the recently proposed projection method [M. Sánchez Sánchez et al., Phys. Rev. B 111, 205133 (2025)] for magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (MATBG) across various symmetry-breaking phases at charge neutrality. The flat-band projected solutions agree well with the full tight binding, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035155] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026

The flat-band projected solutions agree well with the full tight binding, …

item.title: Metal-insulator transition in a boundary three chain model
item.description:
Author(s): Niels John, Yuval Gefen, and Bernd Rosenow

We study the boundary physics of topologially trivial Mott insulators, representing the boundary by three coupled Hubbard chains in a linear confining potential. In the Hartree-Fock approximation, the ground state at and slightly off the particle-hole symmetric point remains insulating even at large…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035156] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Niels John, Yuval Gefen, and Bernd Rosenow

We study the boundary physics of topologially trivial Mott insulators, representing the boundary by three coupled Hubbard chains in a linear confining potential. In the Hartree-Fock approximation, the ground state at and slightly off the particle-hole symmetric point remains insulating even at large…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035156] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026

item.title: Probing quantum geometric nonlinear magnetization via second-harmonic magneto-optical Kerr effect
item.description:
Author(s): Xuan Qian, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Wenkai Zhu, Yuqing Huang, Yiyuan Chen, Hai-Zhou Lu, Yang Ji, and Kaiyou Wang

Quantum geometry provides an intrinsic framework for characterizing the geometric structure of quantum states. It highlights its relevance to various aspects of fundamental physics. However, its direct implications for magnetic phenomena remain largely unexplored. Here, we report the observation of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041407] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xuan Qian, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Wenkai Zhu, Yuqing Huang, Yiyuan Chen, Hai-Zhou Lu, Yang Ji, and Kaiyou Wang

Quantum geometry provides an intrinsic framework for characterizing the geometric structure of quantum states. It highlights its relevance to various aspects of fundamental physics. However, its direct implications for magnetic phenomena remain largely unexplored. Here, we report the observation of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041407] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Probing quantum geometric nonlinear magnetization via second-harmonic magneto-optical Kerr effect
